name: Lemon Roll (Low Fat)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup frozen egg substitute, thawed (or 2 eggs)
recipeInstructions:
1. LIGHT LEMON FILLING: In a small saucepan, combine 2/3 cup sugar and 3 tablespoons cornstarch. Stir in 2/3 cup water. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly until the mixture becomes translucent, thickens and bubbles (6 to 8 minutes). Continue cooking for 1 minute longer. Remove from heat.
2. In a small bowl, beat 1/2 of the hot mixture into an egg yolk. Stir back into the saucepan. Cook, stirring, for 1 minute longer. Stir in lemon juice and grated lemon zest. Pour into a small bowl. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface. Refrigerate at least 3 hours, or until very cold.
3. CAKE: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Coat a 10 x 15-inch jelly roll pan with cooking spray. Line the bottom of the pan with wax paper and coat again with cooking spray.
4. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt on a sheet of wax paper. In another small bowl, beat egg whites with cream of tartar until firm peaks form; set aside.
5. In a separate electric mixer bowl, beat the egg substitute until thick and creamy (about 1 minute). Gradually add sugar, beating constantly until very thick (about 2 minutes).
6. Stir in water and vanilla. Add the flour mixture, stirring until combined. Fold in beaten egg whites. Spread batter evenly in the prepared pan.
7. Bake for 12 to 13 minutes or until the cake just begins to pull away from the sides of the pan and springs back when lightly pressed with a small metal spatula. Loosen the edges with a knife, invert the pan onto a clean kitchen towel dusted with powdered sugar, peel off the wax paper, and gently roll up the cake and towel together from the short end.
8. Place the roll, seam side down, on a wire rack to cool completely. When cool, unroll the cake carefully. Spread with Light Lemon Filling. To start rerolling, lift the end of the cake with the towel. Place the roll, seam side down, on a serving platter and dust with remaining powdered sugar.
